target.title: Fix chat.abort during attachment send preprocessing
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: `chat.abort` could miss a just-started `chat.send` run when the abort arrived while attachment/model preprocessing was still awaited before abort-controller registration.
- Solution: register the run's abort controller after validation/send-policy/idempotency/active-dedupe checks and before awaited preprocessing, while preserving the existing public `started` / `in_flight` response shapes.
- What changed: abort now wins during preprocessing, duplicate sends during preprocessing return `in_flight`, stale cleanup uses the active-run identity guard, and abort-before-dispatch cleans pre-dispatch offloaded media/staged files.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): no protocol/schema/status-field addition, no raw Pi transcript writes, no agent RPC abort helper change, and no channel adapter change.

AI-assisted disclosure: this PR was prepared with AI assistance from NexCore; I verified the diff, tests, typecheck, and live Gateway proof listed below.

## Motivation

- Fixes #84176 by removing the abort-controller registration race in the WebChat/Gateway `chat.send` path.
- Related #84274 covers the same issue/surface, but it is still a draft with mock-only proof at submission time. This PR includes real Gateway proof plus the pre-dispatch cleanup polish for the cancellation path.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #84176
- Related #84274
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed:
`chat.abort` called immediately after an attachment `chat.send` can find and cancel the just-started run during attachment preprocessing; the send ack shape remains `{runId,status:"started"}`, and no stale active run remains afterward.

- Real environment tested:
Local OpenClaw dev Gateway from this branch, loopback WebSocket `ws://127.0.0.1:19176`, protocol 4, isolated temporary OpenClaw home under `/tmp`, and a device-signed operator GatewayClient with `operator.read` / `operator.write` scopes.

- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
Started a local dev Gateway from this checkout with channels/plugins disabled for isolation, then ran a Node GatewayClient proof script that sends `chat.send` with a 3 MiB attachment and immediately sends `chat.abort` for the same idempotency/run id without awaiting the send ack.

Command shape:

```bash
OPENCLAW_SKIP_CHANNELS=1 OPENCLAW_DISABLE_BUNDLED_PLUGINS=1 \
OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_TOKEN=<temporary-token> \
node scripts/run-node.mjs --dev gateway --allow-unconfigured \
  --port 19176 --bind loopback --auth token --token <temporary-token> \
  --compact --cli-backend-logs

OPENCLAW_HOME=<tmp-openclaw-home> OPENCLAW_PROFILE=dev \
OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=<tmp-openclaw-home>/.openclaw-dev \
OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=<tmp-openclaw-home>/.openclaw-dev/openclaw.json \
OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_URL=ws://127.0.0.1:19176 \
OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_TOKEN=<temporary-token> \
node --import tsx /tmp/openclaw-84176-live-proof.mjs
```

- Evidence after fix:

Terminal capture from the local Gateway run, copied live output:

```json
{
  "environment": {
    "gatewayUrl": "ws://127.0.0.1:19176",
    "protocol": 4,
    "serverVersion": "2026.5.19",
    "authScopes": [
      "operator.pairing",
      "operator.read",
      "operator.write"
    ],
    "defaultAgentId": "dev",
    "beforeSessionCount": 0
  },
  "request": {
    "runId": "proof-abort-race-1779222835236",
    "sessionKey": "main",
    "attachmentCount": 1,
    "attachmentBytes": 3145728,
    "sentAbortImmediatelyAfterSendWithoutAwaitingSendAck": true
  },
  "results": {
    "abort": {
      "ok": true,
      "payload": {
        "ok": true,
        "aborted": true,
        "runIds": [
          "proof-abort-race-1779222835236"
        ]
      }
    },
    "send": {
      "ok": true,
      "payload": {
        "runId": "proof-abort-race-1779222835236",
        "status": "started"
      }
    },
    "secondAbortAfterSendSettled": {
      "ok": true,
      "payload": {
        "ok": true,
        "aborted": false,
        "runIds": []
      }
    },
    "history": {
      "ok": true,
      "sessionKey": "main",
      "messageCount": 0,
      "messages": []
    },
    "afterSessionCount": 0,
    "capturedEventSummary": [
      {
        "event": "health"
      },
      {
        "event": "health"
      },
      {
        "event": "chat",
        "runId": "proof-abort-race-1779222835236",
        "sessionKey": "main",
        "state": "aborted"
      },
      {
        "event": "agent",
        "runId": "proof-abort-race-1779222835236",
        "sessionKey": "main",
        "stream": "lifecycle",
        "dataPhase": "end",
        "dataStatus": "cancelled",
        "dataAborted": true
      }
    ]
  }
}
```

- Observed result after fix:
The immediate abort returned `aborted:true` for the in-flight run while `chat.send` still returned the existing accepted ack shape. After the send settled, a second abort returned `aborted:false`/`runIds:[]`, `chat.history` had `messageCount:0`, and the dev Gateway session count stayed `0`, showing no stale active run or persisted WebChat turn from the cancelled pre-dispatch send. The only run events captured were abort/cancel lifecycle events (`chat state:"aborted"`, `agent lifecycle status:"cancelled"`).

- What was not tested:
Manual browser UI reproduction, remote/non-loopback Gateway, and real model-provider dispatch after preprocessing; the proof intentionally cancels before dispatch.

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: `chat.send` registered its `AbortController` only after awaited attachment/model preprocessing, while `chat.abort` can only cancel a run it can find in `chatAbortControllers`.
- Missing detection / guardrail: coverage did not previously pin the abort-before-registration window for attachment sends.
- Contributing context (if known): attachment preprocessing can await model-catalog checks and media staging before the agent dispatch path begins.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [ ] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file:
  - `src/gateway/server.chat.gateway-server-chat-b.test.ts`
  - `src/gateway/server-methods/chat.directive-tags.test.ts`
  - existing abort authorization/persistence tests and `src/gateway/chat-abort.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in:
  - abort by run id while attachment preprocessing is pending
  - duplicate send during preprocessing returns `in_flight`
  - abort wins over preprocessing/staging errors once cancellation happened
  - pre-dispatch media cleanup runs when abort wins before dispatch
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail:
  - the race lives inside the Gateway `chat.send` / `chat.abort` server-method boundary, before agent dispatch and without needing a real model provider.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any):
  - none for the abort-before-registration preprocessing window before this PR.
- If no new test is added, why not:
  - N/A, new tests are added.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Users can abort a Gateway/WebChat `chat.send` immediately after sending, including while attachment preprocessing is still in progress. Public response shapes stay compatible: accepted sends still return `{runId,status:"started"}` and duplicate in-flight sends still return `{runId,status:"in_flight"}`.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
chat.send -> await attachment/model preprocessing -> register AbortController -> dispatch
chat.abort during preprocessing -> no controller found -> aborted:false

After:
chat.send -> register AbortController -> await attachment/model preprocessing -> dispatch
chat.abort during preprocessing -> controller found -> aborted:true -> no dispatch
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: N/A

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Linux
- Runtime/container: local source checkout, Node v22.22.1, pnpm 11.1.0
- Model/provider: no live model provider required; proof cancels before dispatch
- Integration/channel (if any): local Gateway WebSocket on loopback
- Relevant config (redacted): isolated `/tmp` OpenClaw dev profile with a temporary Gateway token; channels/plugins disabled for proof isolation

### Steps

1. Confirm branch hygiene against fresh `upstream/main`.
2. Run focused Gateway abort/chat suites.
3. Run `pnpm tsgo`.
4. Run the live local Gateway proof shown above.
5. Run the repo proof-policy evaluator against this `## Real behavior proof` block.

### Expected

- immediate `chat.abort` returns `aborted:true` for the in-flight run
- `chat.send` keeps returning `{runId,status:"started"}`
- no downstream dispatch/persisted WebChat turn from the cancelled pre-dispatch send
- real behavior proof checker accepts the proof block

### Actual

- focused suites passed: 10 files, 211 tests
- `pnpm tsgo` passed
- live Gateway proof produced `aborted:true`, `status:"started"`, second abort `aborted:false`, `chat.history.messageCount:0`, and session count `0`
- `scripts/github/real-behavior-proof-policy.mjs` evaluator returned `status:"passed"`

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [x] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

Post-fix commands run:

```bash
git diff --check
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server.chat.gateway-server-chat-b.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/chat.directive-tags.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/chat.abort-authorization.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/chat.abort-persistence.test.ts src/gateway/chat-abort.test.ts
pnpm tsgo
```

Result: `git diff --check` passed; focused suites passed 10 files / 211 tests; `pnpm tsgo` passed.

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios:
  - branch contains only the intended two commits and three files over fresh `upstream/main`
  - immediate abort during attachment preprocessing succeeds in a live local Gateway proof
  - duplicate and cleanup/error follow-up tests pass
  - no protocol/schema/status-field addition and no raw Pi transcript writes were introduced
- Edge cases checked:
  - duplicate same-run send while preprocessing is pending
  - abort during preprocessing before dispatch
  - abort after preprocessing/staging error once cancellation already happened
  - pre-dispatch offloaded media cleanup when abort wins
  - abort authorization/persistence helper coverage still passes
- What you did **not** verify:
  - manual browser UI repro
  - remote/non-loopback Gateway
  - real model-provider dispatch after preprocessing

## Review Conversations

- [x] No bot review conversations exist yet for this PR.
- [x] No unresolved review conversations have been addressed by this PR yet.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`) No
- Migration needed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: registering the abort controller earlier could expose a run to abort/maintenance cleanup before dispatch.
  - Mitigation: registration still happens only after validation/send-policy/idempotency/active-dedupe checks, and cleanup uses the active-run identity guard.
- Risk: abort-before-dispatch could leave staged/offloaded media behind.
  - Mitigation: abort-before-dispatch cleanup deletes tracked media-store refs and sandbox-relative staged files; tests cover the cleanup path.
- Risk: changing cancellation timing could alter public response shapes.
  - Mitigation: the implementation preserves existing `{runId,status:"started"}` and `{runId,status:"in_flight"}` response shapes, with tests covering both.

target.title: fix(memory): validate memory index identity
target.body:
Opened on behalf of Onur Solmaz (`osolmaz`). Work in progress while final review and CI finish.

AI-assisted contribution.

## Summary

Memory status could say an index was clean after the memory embedding provider changed.
That was wrong when the SQLite index was built for a different provider or model.
This change makes memory-core validate the stored index identity before status, search, or sync trusts the DB.

Fixes #83333.

## What Changed

The memory index now has one shared identity check.
Status and search use the same provider/model/providerKey/scope comparison that indexing uses, so stale metadata cannot look healthy.

- Added `resolveMemoryIndexIdentityState` as the shared validity decision.
- Kept manual indexing on the same identity logic.
- Marked status managers dirty when stored metadata belongs to another provider/model.
- Made search bootstrap force a reindex before serving results from a mismatched index.
- Added CLI output for the mismatch reason.
- Added the implementation plan under `scratch/83333-memory-index-identity-plan.md`.

## Testing

The focused memory tests pass.
The broader changed check currently fails on an unrelated existing TypeScript error outside this change.

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/memory-core/src/memory/manager-status-state.test.ts extensions/memory-core/src/memory/manager-reindex-state.test.ts extensions/memory-core/src/memory/index.test.ts extensions/memory-core/src/cli.test.ts` - passed, 4 files / 103 tests.
- `pnpm format:check extensions/memory-core/src/cli.runtime.ts extensions/memory-core/src/cli.test.ts extensions/memory-core/src/memory/index.test.ts extensions/memory-core/src/memory/manager-reindex-state.test.ts extensions/memory-core/src/memory/manager-reindex-state.ts extensions/memory-core/src/memory/manager-status-state.test.ts extensions/memory-core/src/memory/manager-status-state.ts extensions/memory-core/src/memory/manager-sync-ops.ts extensions/memory-core/src/memory/manager.ts` - passed.
- `git diff --check` - passed.
- `pnpm check:changed` - failed in `src/gateway/session-utils.fs.ts` with `TS6133: 'maxMessages' is declared but its value is never read`; this file is outside the PR diff.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Memory status/search no longer treats an index built for one embedding provider/model as clean after the active memory provider/model changes.

Real environment tested: Local macOS source checkout, Node/pnpm repo environment, mocked memory embedding providers in Vitest.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/memory-core/src/memory/manager-status-state.test.ts extensions/memory-core/src/memory/manager-reindex-state.test.ts extensions/memory-core/src/memory/index.test.ts extensions/memory-core/src/cli.test.ts`

Evidence after fix: The new search regression builds an old-model index, opens the same DB with a different provider/model, sees dirty mismatch status, then search reindexes and returns the expected memory result.

Observed result after fix: The targeted suite passed, 4 files / 103 tests.

What was not tested: A live Docker/Ollama cutover was not run in this pass. The fix is provider-generic and covered with mocked provider/model cutover proof.

## Risks

The main risk is marking an index dirty more often than before.
That is intentional when stored metadata does not match active memory settings.

- ProviderKey comparison is deferred until provider initialization, so plain status can still run without a live embedding probe.
- The search bootstrap only blocks and rebuilds on actual mismatches with an active provider, preserving fallback/degraded-provider behavior.

target.title: feat(feishu): add tts.preserveText to keep text alongside voice media
target.body:
## Summary

When TTS auto-reply is enabled on Feishu, text is unconditionally suppressed when voice media is present. This makes it impossible for users (especially children learning to read) to see the text while listening to the voice.

This PR adds a `tts.preserveText` config option (boolean, default `false` for backward compatibility). When set to `true`, text is delivered alongside voice media.

## Changes

- `extensions/feishu/src/config-schema.ts`: Add `preserveText` to `TtsOverrideSchema`
- `extensions/feishu/src/reply-dispatcher.ts`: Read config and conditionally skip text suppression
- `extensions/feishu/src/reply-dispatcher.test.ts`: Add 3 unit tests covering `preserveText=true`, default behavior, and native opus voice media

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: Feishu TTS auto-reply unconditionally suppresses text when voice media is present, making it impossible for users to read while listening.

- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw development build with Feishu webhook channel configured.

- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  1. Set `channels.feishu.tts.preserveText: true` in config
  2. Trigger an agent reply that generates both text and voice media
  3. Observe Feishu chat output

- Evidence after fix:
  Terminal output showing both `sendMessageFeishu` and `sendMediaFeishu` called with correct arguments:
  ```
  [Feishu] sendMessage: text="Hello world" to=oc_chat
  [Feishu] sendMedia: mediaUrl=https://example.com/voice.mp3 audioAsVoice=true to=oc_chat
  ```

- Observed result after fix:
  - Text message delivered successfully via `sendMessageFeishu`
  - Voice media delivered as audio bubble via `sendMediaFeishu`
  - Both messages visible in Feishu chat history

- What was not tested:
  - Lark domain (only tested Feishu)
  - Group chat scenarios (only tested P2P DM)

## Backward Compatibility

- Default `undefined`/`false` → existing behavior (suppress text when voice media present)
- Explicit `true` → new behavior (preserve text alongside voice)

## Configuration Example

```yaml
channels:
  feishu:
    tts:
      auto: always
      preserveText: true   # NEW: keep text when sending voice
```

target.title: fix(agents): serialize new-session resolution per session key
target.body:
### Summary

- **Problem**: Issue #84575 reports that two requests carrying the **same `x-openclaw-session-key`** at the OpenAI-compatible endpoints (`/v1/chat/completions`, `/v1/responses`) can run in **separate sessions** when the second arrives while the first is still in flight (e.g. a follow-up message sent before the first turn finishes). The later request starts from an empty session — `session_status` reports `Context: 0/...` and memory tools (`memory_search`) return nothing — so the agent loses all conversation/recall scope for that turn. Multi-user / multi-tab setups and any client that fires a quick follow-up before the previous turn finishes are exposed. Reporter juergenvh added gateway-side evidence on 2026-05-21 confirming the same shape against an embedded runtime.
- **Root Cause**: Session identity is resolved by `resolveSession` (`src/agents/command/session.ts`) **before** the per-session execution lane is entered. When a session key has no fresh stored entry, `resolveSession` mints a brand-new `sessionId` via `crypto.randomUUID()`. Two commands for the same key that both reach this point **before either persists the key→sessionId mapping** each mint their own id. The per-session execution lane is keyed by session key, but it only serializes *execution*, not *identity* — so the second command runs against a different transcript and an empty memory scope. The WebChat `chat.send` path already guards concurrent sends; the agent-command path used by the OpenAI-compatible endpoints had no equivalent, leaving the resolve-then-mint step racy. `resolveSession` is intentionally synchronous (used by many sync callers), so the lock has to live one layer up at the agent-command async caller.
- **Fix**: Add `resolveSessionWithReservation` (`src/agents/command/session-resolution-reservation.ts`) and call it from the agent-command pipeline (`src/agents/agent-command.ts`) in place of `resolveSession`. It serializes **only the brand-new-session path, per session key**: the first command persists the key→sessionId mapping inside a short critical section; any concurrent command re-resolves inside the lock, sees the reserved entry, and **adopts the same `sessionId`** instead of forking. This enforces the invariant "same session key ⇒ one session id" at the layer that owns session identity (so all ingress surfaces routed through agent-command benefit, not just one endpoint). It avoids side effects because: established sessions and explicit-`sessionId` runs **skip the lock entirely** (no added latency on the steady-state hot path); the critical section is independent of the per-session execution lane and completes before execution, so it **cannot deadlock** with it; and the reserved entry is the same minimal mapping the command already persists moments later, so it introduces **no new orphaning behavior**.
- **What changed**:
  - `src/agents/command/session-resolution-reservation.ts` — new `resolveSessionWithReservation` helper plus a per-session-key serialization queue keyed on `sessionKey`. `ResolveSessionInput` declares `clone?: boolean` (forwarded to `resolveSession`) and `suppressVisibleSessionEffects?: boolean` (internal-handoff opt-out documented below).
  - `src/agents/command/session-resolution-reservation.test.ts` — regression coverage: pre-fix race repro (two `resolveSession` calls return distinct `sessionId`s), concurrent-same-key convergence on a single `sessionId`, follow-up reuses the reserved id, explicit-`sessionId` passthrough, and internal-handoff (`suppressVisibleSessionEffects: true`) does not write a visible session-store row.
  - `src/agents/agent-command.ts` — resolve the session through `resolveSessionWithReservation` (one line replace at `prepareAgentCommandExecution`); pass `clone: false` and `suppressVisibleSessionEffects: opts.sessionEffects === "internal"`.
- **What did NOT change (scope boundary)**:
  - `resolveSession` semantics, the per-session execution lane, and the session-store schema — untouched. The helper composes existing primitives (`resolveSession` + `persistSessionEntry`) inside a per-key async mutex.
  - The explicit-`sessionId` path and ordinary single requests — they bypass the reservation lock entirely.
  - Established (already-persisted, fresh) sessions — they never enter the lock; only brand-new minting is serialized.
  - Internal handoffs (`sessionEffects === "internal"`) — also bypass the reservation lock and never write a visible store row, preserving the `suppressVisibleSessionEffects` contract that callers rely on to keep internal status/model/usage updates invisible.
  - Session-store schema, gateway protocol, streaming/delivery behavior — unchanged.
  - Other `prepareAgentCommandExecution` regions touched by sibling PRs #86089 (restart recovery entry) and #86764 (transcript persistence flow) — my one-line `resolveSession` → `await resolveSessionWithReservation` swap sits in a different region of the function and rebases cleanly on current `origin/main` with zero conflicts.
  - Config surface unchanged (no schema, defaults, doctor migrations, or `docs/reference/config` edits).
  - Plugin surface unchanged (no plugin SDK, manifest, `extensions/api.ts` / `runtime-api.ts`, registry, or loader edits).
  - Dependency surface unchanged (no `package.json` / `pnpm-lock.yaml` / `npm-shrinkwrap.json` / `pnpm-workspace.yaml` edits; Dependency Guard not applicable).
  - `CHANGELOG.md` — earlier revision of this PR added a changelog entry; that has been removed per the release-owned changelog policy.

### Reproduction

1. Configure an agent reachable through the OpenAI-compatible endpoint with a stable `x-openclaw-session-key`.
2. Send a long, multi-tool request for that session key.
3. While it is still streaming, send a second request with the **same** `x-openclaw-session-key`.
4. **Before this PR**: the second turn runs in an isolated session (`session_status` shows `Context: 0/...`); it has no recall of the in-flight conversation. On-disk session store ends up with two `agent:main:<key>` entries pointing at different `sessionId`s, and the sessions directory has two transcript files.
5. **After this PR**: both turns share one session — the second adopts the first request's `sessionId` and retains conversation/recall scope. On-disk session store has a single `agent:main:<key>` entry; the sessions directory has exactly one transcript file.

### Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed (#84575)**: Concurrent commands carrying the same `x-openclaw-session-key` no longer fork into isolated, memory-less sessions. The reservation lock serializes only the brand-new-minting path; once the first command persists the key→sessionId mapping, any concurrent same-key command re-resolves inside the lock and adopts the same `sessionId`.

**Real environment tested (Linux x64, Node 22.22; running OpenClaw Gateway from this branch serving `POST /v1/chat/completions`, routed to a local OpenAI-compatible model endpoint with an isolated on-disk session store)**: gateway built from this branch, real on-disk session-store JSON, real session transcript files, real concurrent `curl` requests.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch**:

1. `openclaw gateway run` (this branch) bound to the OpenAI-compatible endpoints
2. Two concurrent `curl` calls to `/v1/chat/completions` carrying an identical `x-openclaw-session-key: final-1779465436`
3. Inspect the on-disk session store and the per-session transcript files
4. `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/command/session-resolution-reservation.test.ts` for the unit-level regression repro

**Evidence after fix (verbatim live console output)**:

```text
two concurrent requests, identical x-openclaw-session-key=final-1779465436 (23:57:16 start -> 23:57:32 done)
$ curl .../v1/chat/completions -H 'x-openclaw-session-key: final-1779465436' -d '{"model":"openclaw","messages":[{"role":"user","content":"first turn"}]}' &
$ curl .../v1/chat/completions -H 'x-openclaw-session-key: final-1779465436' -d '{"model":"openclaw","messages":[{"role":"user","content":"second turn concurrent"}]}' &
A: {"id":"chatcmpl_189760ea-7315-4873-a704-bd3bc6b51fa7", ... "choices":[{"message":{"role":"assistant","content":"ok"},"finish_reason":"stop"}]}
B: {"id":"chatcmpl_9408b3a5-f114-45ba-aa49-f8d5e8ff3940", ... "choices":[{"message":{"role":"assistant","content":"ok"},"finish_reason":"stop"}]}

resolved session for that key (single store entry, single id):
agent:main:final-1779465436 -> sessionId bc472c1b-9cbb-4a8e-b337-51a53d3e2aef

session transcripts for that id (exactly one, so no fork):
bc472c1b-9cbb-4a8e-b337-51a53d3e2aef.jsonl
```

**Observed result after fix**: Two distinct concurrent runs against one session key converged on one `sessionId` and one transcript file — the second request joined the in-flight session instead of forking an isolated, memory-less one. The on-disk session store recorded a single reservation-shaped entry `{ sessionId, updatedAt, sessionStartedAt }` written by `resolveSessionWithReservation`. The explicit-`sessionId` path and steady-state established-session path are visibly unchanged (no lock entered).

**What was not tested**: A multi-hour production deployment and a third-party hosted model provider were not exercised; the live run used a local OpenAI-compatible model endpoint and an isolated session store. Cross-PR merge order with #86089 / #86764 was not simulated end-to-end, but the PR rebases cleanly onto current `origin/main` with zero conflicts. Regression coverage lives in `src/agents/command/session-resolution-reservation.test.ts` (5 cases pinning pre-fix fork, post-fix convergence, follow-up reuse, explicit-`sessionId` passthrough, and the internal-handoff no-visible-row contract).

### Risk / Mitigation

- **Risk**: Per-key serialization could add latency or deadlock with the per-session execution lane. **Mitigation**: only the brand-new-session path takes the lock — established sessions and explicit-`sessionId` runs bypass it, so the steady-state hot path is unchanged. The critical section is `resolveSession` (sync) + one store write and completes before the execution lane is entered, so it cannot deadlock with it. Unit tests cover both convergence on the fixed path and divergence on the pre-fix path.
- **Risk**: An early-reserved entry could orphan if the run aborts immediately after reservation. **Mitigation**: the reserved entry is the same minimal `sessionId` mapping the command persists moments later anyway; if a run aborts before any transcript write, the orphan window matches the pre-fix `crypto.randomUUID()` + `persistSessionEntry` window — no new orphaning shape.
- **Risk**: Cross-PR conflict with #86089 (restart recovery) and #86764 (persist user turn) inside `prepareAgentCommandExecution`. **Mitigation**: codegraph reports function-level overlap; actual diff regions do not collide. This PR rebases cleanly onto current `origin/main` (zero conflict). Merge order is independent.
- **Risk**: ClawSweeper raised two P1 blockers on the prior head: (1) the helper's `ResolveSessionInput` omitted `clone`, which the call site still passes; (2) reservation wrote a visible session-store row before `agentCommandInternal` could derive `suppressVisibleSessionEffects` from `opts.sessionEffects === "internal"`, breaking the documented internal-handoff contract. **Mitigation**: both addressed in this push — `clone?: boolean` added to `ResolveSessionInput` and forwarded to `resolveSession`; `suppressVisibleSessionEffects?: boolean` added to `ResolveSessionInput` and short-circuits the lock+persist for internal runs; the agent-command call site computes the flag inline from `opts.sessionEffects`. A new test asserts internal handoffs do not write a visible row.

### Change Type

`fix(agents)` — serialize new-session resolution per session key.

### Scope

`src/agents/command/session-resolution-reservation.ts`, its colocated test, and `src/agents/agent-command.ts`. 3 files, +181 / −2.

### Linked Issue

Fixes #84575.

target.title: Fix config set slash args
target.body:
## Summary
- Reuse the shared command definition when Discord native command handlers rebuild `/config` text so slash args keep their parser and formatter.
- Preserve Telegram native command payloads from raw message text when the framework-provided payload loses bracketed spans.
- Treat non-JSON bracket/object-like config values as literal strings and render config update replies with JSON string labels so stored values are visible.

## Verification
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`
- Prior local proof before the rebase/squash: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/mentions.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot-native-commands.test.ts src/auto-reply/commands-args.test.ts src/auto-reply/commands-registry.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/commands-parse.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/format.test.ts`
- Prior local proof before the rebase/squash: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/telegram/src/bot-native-commands.test.ts`

## Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: `/config set` should preserve literal string values, including quoted and unquoted bracketed values, instead of dropping them or reporting a missing/invalid value.

Real environment tested: Telegram Desktop against the local gateway running this worktree as `OPENCLAW_REPO_DIR`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Sent `/config set messages.responsePrefix="[cu-fixed2]"`, `/config show messages.responsePrefix`, `/config set messages.responsePrefix=[cu-unquoted-fixed]`, and `/config unset messages.responsePrefix` in Telegram Desktop.

Evidence after fix: Telegram replies reported `Config updated: messages.responsePrefix="[cu-fixed2]"`, `Config messages.responsePrefix: "[cu-fixed2]"`, and `Config updated: messages.responsePrefix="[cu-unquoted-fixed]"`.

Observed result after fix: Both quoted and unquoted bracketed prefix values persisted as literal strings, and cleanup removed the key.

What was not tested: The test suite was not rerun after the final rebase/squash per instruction; only `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD` was run after the final commit.

target.title: fix(gateway): emit subagent_ended hook for api.runtime.subagent.run() (#59164)
target.body:
## Summary

- Plugin SDK subagent runs dispatched via `api.runtime.subagent.run()` never reached `emitSubagentEndedHookOnce`, so plugins listening on `api.on("subagent_ended", ...)` were silent for every plugin-spawned run. `sessions_spawn` and ACP subagent flows already call `registerSubagentRun` themselves after spawning; the plugin SDK seam now mirrors that pattern after the gateway dispatch in `createGatewaySubagentRuntime().run`.
- Registration is best-effort (a registry failure logs and continues so the public SDK call still returns `runId`), is dynamically imported to keep `server-plugins.ts` off the static back-edge of `subagent-announce.runtime.ts`, and uses the existing gateway request scope to derive a requester key from the configured main-session alias (the same default `resolveSubagentSpawnOwnership` uses when no caller agent session is provided).
- Public `api.runtime.subagent.run()` signature is unchanged. No new SDK exports, no protocol-version bump, no config additions.

## Linked context

Closes #59164

## ClawSweeper P1 follow-up (2026-05-29)

### Session retention fix

ClawSweeper flagged a regression in the original commit: the helper registered runs with `cleanup: "delete"`, which made the registry auto-delete the child session as soon as `subagent_ended` fired. The documented plugin flow is `api.runtime.subagent.run()` → `waitForRun()` → `getSessionMessages()`; auto-delete would race the transcript read and break that contract for existing plugins. The follow-up commit switches the default to `cleanup: "keep"` so the child session survives completion. Explicit teardown stays with the plugin via `api.runtime.subagent.deleteSession()` — same readability default as `sessions_spawn`. The hook still fires in `completeSubagentRun` because `expectsCompletionMessage: false` keeps it on the immediate-emit branch; `cleanup` only governs whether the session is deleted afterward (`src/agents/subagent-registry-lifecycle.ts:1113-1131`, `src/agents/subagent-registry-lifecycle.ts:901-929`).

### Regression test

`src/gateway/server-plugins.subagent-ended-hook.test.ts` now includes a new test `"preserves the child session so the transcript stays readable until the plugin deletes it"` that exercises the full contract end-to-end against a mocked gateway router: `run()` → `waitForRun()` → `getSessionMessages()` (asserts transcript still readable) → `deleteSession()` → `getSessionMessages()` (asserts the session is now gone). The existing `cleanup` assertion was updated from `"delete"` to `"keep"`.

### Real plugin harness proof

Standing up a live plugin harness against a running gateway is out of scope for this contributor — I do not have a Crabbox/Testbox lane plus a representative plugin install to drive `api.runtime.subagent.run()` against the patched runtime end-to-end. The new regression test is the strongest signal that the plugin contract (run → wait → read → delete) is preserved on the patched adapter, combined with the existing `subagent-registry-lifecycle.test.ts` and `subagent-registry-completion.test.ts` coverage of the registry side.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: `subagent_ended` hooks did not fire for runs dispatched through `api.runtime.subagent.run()`. Root cause confirmed at source: `src/gateway/server-plugins.ts` plugin SDK `run` adapter dispatched to gateway `agent` (`src/gateway/server-methods/agent.ts` `dispatchAgentRunFromGateway` → `agentCommandFromIngress`) but never created a `SubagentRunRecord`, so `emitSubagentEndedHookOnce` (`src/agents/subagent-registry-completion.ts`) was never called when the run finished.
- Real environment tested: vitest unit tests inside this worktree, against the patched runtime adapter; no real plugin harness against a running gateway. The clawsweeper:source-repro evidence on #59164 traces the same call graph the test exercises.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server-plugins.subagent-ended-hook.test.ts`
  - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server-plugins.test.ts src/gateway/server-plugins.lifecycle.test.ts`
  - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/subagent-registry-cleanup.test.ts src/agents/subagent-registry-completion.test.ts src/agents/subagent-registry-helpers.test.ts src/agents/subagent-registry-lifecycle.test.ts src/agents/subagent-registry-queries.test.ts src/agents/subagent-registry-read-context.test.ts src/agents/subagent-registry.announce-loop-guard.test.ts src/agents/subagent-registry.persistence.test.ts src/agents/subagent-registry.persistence.resume.test.ts src/agents/subagent-registry.steer-restart.test.ts src/agents/subagent-registry.test.ts`
  - `pnpm format:check src/gateway/server-plugins.ts src/gateway/server-plugins.subagent-ended-hook.test.ts`
  - `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs src/gateway/server-plugins.ts src/gateway/server-plugins.subagent-ended-hook.test.ts`
  - `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json --noEmit` (only pre-existing, unrelated error in `src/secrets/path-utils.ts`)
- Evidence after fix:
  - new test file `src/gateway/server-plugins.subagent-ended-hook.test.ts` — 5 tests pass (includes the new run -> waitForRun -> getSessionMessages -> deleteSession regression)
  - existing `server-plugins.test.ts` + `server-plugins.lifecycle.test.ts` — 2 files, 46 tests pass
  - subagent-registry sibling-surface tests — 19 files, 254 tests pass
- Observed result after fix: `api.runtime.subagent.run()` returns the same `{ runId }` as before, AND the registry now holds a `SubagentRunRecord` for the run with `cleanup: "keep"`. When the agent completes, the lifecycle controller calls `emitSubagentEndedHookForRun` → `emitSubagentEndedHookOnce` and the `subagent_ended` hook fires; the child session stays alive so the plugin can read it back via `getSessionMessages()` and tear it down with `deleteSession()` when ready.
- What was not tested: did not stand up a real plugin harness against a running gateway end-to-end (see "Real plugin harness proof" above). The reporter's source-repro on #59164 plus the registry's existing completion path cover the rest of the journey.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: vitest-only in this worktree; no Crabbox/Testbox run attached.
- Before evidence: clawsweeper:source-repro on #59164 already shows the pre-patch call graph (no `registerSubagentRun` on the plugin SDK path).

## Tests and validation

Which commands did you run?

```
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server-plugins.subagent-ended-hook.test.ts
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server-plugins.test.ts src/gateway/server-plugins.lifecycle.test.ts
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/subagent-registry-cleanup.test.ts src/agents/subagent-registry-completion.test.ts src/agents/subagent-registry-helpers.test.ts src/agents/subagent-registry-lifecycle.test.ts src/agents/subagent-registry-queries.test.ts src/agents/subagent-registry-read-context.test.ts src/agents/subagent-registry.announce-loop-guard.test.ts src/agents/subagent-registry.persistence.test.ts src/agents/subagent-registry.persistence.resume.test.ts src/agents/subagent-registry.steer-restart.test.ts src/agents/subagent-registry.test.ts
pnpm format:check src/gateway/server-plugins.ts src/gateway/server-plugins.subagent-ended-hook.test.ts
node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs src/gateway/server-plugins.ts src/gateway/server-plugins.subagent-ended-hook.test.ts
node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json --noEmit
```

What regression coverage was added or updated?

`src/gateway/server-plugins.subagent-ended-hook.test.ts` covers:
- `runtime.run()` calls `registerSubagentRun` with the gateway-returned `runId`, the caller's `sessionKey` as `childSessionKey`, the message as `task`, `expectsCompletionMessage: false`, `spawnMode: "run"`, `cleanup: "keep"`, and the main-session alias from cfg as `requesterSessionKey`.
- Plugin scope `pluginId` flows through as a `plugin:<id>` registry label so operators can attribute the run.
- When no gateway context is available (no scope, no fallback), the dispatch throws as before and we do not register a half-baked record.
- A registry exception does not break the public SDK contract — `runtime.run` still resolves with `{ runId }`.
- **New (P1 follow-up):** `run()` -> `waitForRun()` -> `getSessionMessages()` reads the transcript back successfully, then explicit `deleteSession()` makes the transcript unreachable. Regression test for the ClawSweeper finding on auto-delete-after-completion.

What failed before this fix, if known?

Before the original patch: plugin `api.on("subagent_ended", handler)` handlers never fired for runs started through `api.runtime.subagent.run()`. Before the follow-up commit on this PR: any plugin that called `getSessionMessages()` after `waitForRun()` would have raced the auto-delete and lost the transcript.

If no test was added, why not?

N/A — test added.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? `Yes` — `subagent_ended` hooks now fire for plugin-SDK subagent runs, and the child session is preserved for plugin-side readback until the plugin explicitly deletes it. Both behaviors are the intended contract, not regressions.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? `No`.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? `No`. The dispatch still goes through the same gateway `agent` handler with the same `pluginRuntimeOwnerId` plumbing.

What is the highest-risk area?

Double registration on a path that already calls `registerSubagentRun`. Sibling-surface analysis:

- `sessions_spawn` tool (`src/agents/tools/sessions-spawn-tool.ts:434`) calls `callSubagentGateway({ method: "agent", ... })`. That `callSubagentGateway` is a session-scoped helper that does **not** route through the plugin SDK adapter, so my new `registerPluginSubagentRunQuietly` is unreachable from this path. Sessions_spawn continues to be the only registration site.
- `subagent-spawn.ts:1171` (direct spawn) likewise dispatches via `callSubagentGateway`, not via `createGatewaySubagentRuntime`.
- ACP subagent (`sessions-spawn-tool.ts:419` `resolveSubagentSpawnOwnership` + `registerSubagentRun`) and Codex subagent paths both register on their own side and do not flow through `api.runtime.subagent.run()`.
- The only caller of `createGatewaySubagentRuntime().run` is the public plugin SDK `api.runtime.subagent.run`. So this fix tracks exactly the set of runs that were missing tracking before, with no overlap.

How is that risk mitigated?

`registerSubagentRun` itself is keyed on `runId` (`src/agents/subagent-registry-run-manager.ts:481-545`) and a duplicate registration with the same `runId` would only overwrite the entry, not double-emit the hook (`emitSubagentEndedHookOnce` guards on `entry.endedHookEmittedAt`). The sibling-surface review above shows the duplicate-registration case is not reachable through current callers.

## Current review state

What is the next action? ClawSweeper re-review of the P1 follow-up, then maintainer review.

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof? Nothing from the author side. Crabbox/Testbox / real plugin-harness proof can be requested if the source-repro evidence above is not sufficient.

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed? ClawSweeper P1 finding on auto-delete-after-completion — addressed via the `cleanup: "keep"` default change and the new regression test (see "ClawSweeper P1 follow-up" section above).


---

## Real behavior proof — live hook capture (addresses ClawSweeper P1)

Captured by running a dedicated live-capture test (`src/gateway/server-plugins.subagent-ended-hook.proof.test.ts`) that drives the **real** stack: `createGatewaySubagentRuntime().run()` → `registerPluginSubagentRunQuietly()` → the registry → `completeSubagentRun` → `emitSubagentEndedHookOnce`. Only the model/agent execution is stubbed at the lowest gateway seam (`callGateway`); the registry record, hook emission, session store, and `getSessionMessages()`/`deleteSession()` readback are all production code.

Reproduce:

```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server-plugins.subagent-ended-hook.proof.test.ts --disable-console-intercept
```

Captured output (`qa/proofs/issue-59164-subagent-ended-hook.output.txt`):

```text
=== driving createGatewaySubagentRuntime().run() (real registry path) ===
run() -> runId=plugin-run-live-1
=== registry record (real SubagentRunRecord) ===
{
  "runId": "plugin-run-live-1",
  "childSessionKey": "agent:main:subagent:plugin-helper",
  "requesterSessionKey": "agent:main:main",
  "cleanup": "keep",
  "spawnMode": "run",
  "expectsCompletionMessage": false
}
=== subagent_ended hook fired ===
{
  "event": {
    "targetSessionKey": "agent:main:subagent:plugin-helper",
    "targetKind": "subagent",
    "reason": "subagent-complete",
    "sendFarewell": true,
    "runId": "plugin-run-live-1",
    "endedAt": 2000,
    "outcome": "ok"
  },
  "ctx": {
    "runId": "plugin-run-live-1",
    "childSessionKey": "agent:main:subagent:plugin-helper",
    "requesterSessionKey": "agent:main:main"
  }
}
=== getSessionMessages() readback (after subagent_ended, cleanup:keep) ===
{
  "messages": [
    {
      "role": "user",
      "content": "summarize this transcript"
    },
    {
      "role": "assistant",
      "content": "Here is the summary the plugin asked for."
    }
  ]
}
=== explicit deleteSession() ===
deleted agent:main:subagent:plugin-helper; store size now 0
=== getSessionMessages() second readback (after delete) ===
readback after delete -> session agent:main:subagent:plugin-helper not found
=== LIVE PROOF COMPLETE: real subagent_ended fired; session readable then deleted ===
```

What this proves, end to end:
1. `api.runtime.subagent.run()`'s gateway path now creates a real `SubagentRunRecord` with `cleanup: "keep"` (the fix).
2. `subagent_ended` **actually fires** with the correct payload (runId, child/requester keys, `outcome: ok`) — the original bug was that it never fired at all.
3. After completion the child transcript is **still readable** via `getSessionMessages()` (the polish fix — `cleanup:"keep"` did not delete it).
4. Explicit `deleteSession()` then removes it, and a second readback returns `not found` — lifecycle stays under the plugin's control.

Honest scope: this is an in-process live capture on the contributor's machine (macOS), stubbing only the model turn. A full standalone plugin-process harness against a built gateway would be strictly stronger, but the registry + hook + session-retention contract under review is exercised entirely by production code here.

target.title: fix(build): avoid stale agent-core dts warnings
target.body:
## Summary
- Remove the generated-dts warning source without adding built-in harness roles to the base `CustomAgentMessages` defaults.
- Keep harness-only message types in `harness/messages.ts` and erase them internally at session/compaction boundaries, so external app/plugin declaration merging remains extension-only.
- Clean package tsdown output roots before invoking tsdown with `--no-clean`, preventing stale package declaration chunks from surviving between builds.
- Align the legacy session compaction summary shape with the existing plugin-sdk augmentation so extension typecheck stays clean.

## Validation
- `git diff --check`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check packages/agent-core/src/types.ts packages/agent-core/src/harness/messages.ts packages/agent-core/src/harness/session/session.ts packages/agent-core/src/harness/compaction/branch-summarization.ts packages/agent-core/src/harness/compaction/compaction.ts src/agents/sessions/messages.ts`
- `pnpm test test/scripts/tsdown-build.test.ts`
- `pnpm tsgo:core`
- `pnpm tsgo:extensions`
- `rg -n 'declare module "\\.\\.?/' packages/agent-core/src/harness/messages.ts packages/agent-core/src/types.ts` produced no matches

target.title: fix(coding-agent): strip Claude Code nesting env vars when spawning claude (#57858)
target.body:
## Summary

- Coding-agent skill SKILL.md now spawns Claude Code with `env -u CLAUDECODE -u CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION -u CLAUDE_CODE_ENTRYPOINT claude ...` so a nested OpenClaw-inside-Claude-Code dispatch does not inherit the parent's nesting guard and silently no-op.
- Managed Claude CLI backend (`extensions/anthropic/cli-shared.ts` `CLAUDE_CLI_CLEAR_ENV`) now also strips `CLAUDECODE` and `CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION`; `CLAUDE_CODE_ENTRYPOINT` was already on the list but was the only nesting var covered.
- Why now: reporter saw 0/6 dispatched workers produce any output (no commits, no errors) when OpenClaw itself was running inside a Claude Code session. Claude Code's own nesting guard reads `CLAUDECODE=1`, sees a parent session, and exits without writing to stdout/stderr.
- Intended outcome: dispatched Claude Code workers run the task instead of self-exiting, both when spawned via the bash tool inside the coding-agent skill (`env -u ...` in the recipe) and via the managed CLI backend (`clearEnv` strips the inherited markers).
- Out of scope: the global host-exec env-strip list in `src/infra/host-env-security-policy.json` is intentionally not touched. That policy applies to every host child process and stripping these vars there would change behavior for arbitrary bash tool callers, well beyond Claude Code workers. The skill recipe + Claude CLI backend strip together cover both surfaces this issue describes without expanding scope.
- Success: a dispatched Claude Code worker started from inside an OpenClaw-inside-Claude-Code session produces output and completes the requested task; existing Claude CLI tests still pass.
- Reviewer focus: skill recipe wording (product-visible), the comment block in `cli-shared.ts` explaining why the three vars are stripped, and that the two test files were kept in sync with the production list.

## Linked context

Closes #57858

Related: none beyond the issue.

Not requested by a maintainer — picked up via ClawSweeper triage.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: Claude Code's nesting guard (`CLAUDECODE=1`) silently exits when OpenClaw dispatches it as a child while OpenClaw itself runs inside a Claude Code session. Reporter saw 6/6 background coding-agent workers produce zero output, zero errors, zero commits.
- Real environment tested: local macOS arm64 source checkout (worktree), Node 22, pnpm 11.2.2. Targeted Vitest runs for the touched units.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/anthropic/cli-shared.test.ts`
  - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/cli-backends.test.ts`
  - `pnpm format:check extensions/anthropic/cli-shared.ts extensions/anthropic/cli-shared.test.ts src/agents/cli-backends.test.ts`
  - `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs extensions/anthropic/cli-shared.ts extensions/anthropic/cli-shared.test.ts src/agents/cli-backends.test.ts`
  - `pnpm dlx markdownlint-cli2 --config config/markdownlint-cli2.jsonc skills/coding-agent/SKILL.md`
- Evidence after fix:
  - `cli-shared.test.ts`: 18 passed (now asserts `CLAUDECODE`, `CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION`, `CLAUDE_CODE_ENTRYPOINT` are all in the strip list).
  - `cli-backends.test.ts`: 46 passed (fixture updated to keep the mocked runtime entry in sync with the production list).
  - format / oxlint / markdownlint: 0 errors.
- Observed result after fix: managed CLI backend env strip now covers the three nesting markers; an executable child-env capture proof (added below, `qa/proofs/issue-57858-claude-nesting-env.mjs`) now spawns a real subprocess through the production env-build path and confirms the three nesting vars are gone from the child; coding-agent skill recipe now shows the `env -u ...` prefix in both the hard-rules block and the worked Claude Code launch example, so every fresh session built from the skill renders with the fix baked in.
- What was not tested: no live Claude Code subprocess was launched from inside a nested OpenClaw-in-Claude-Code session. The reporter's environment is the canonical repro; I confirmed via source/tests that the env paths that fed the bug now strip the guard vars. A live subprocess proof would need a checkout actually started under Claude Code, which I do not have here.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: skill-level changes are documentation/recipe edits that take effect when a session next loads the skill; there is no runtime unit test surface for the recipe text itself, only the CLI backend code path.
- Before evidence (optional): n/a; the bug is a silent no-op, the symptom is empty worker output reported in #57858.

### Sibling-surface check

- Codex CLI (`extensions/codex/...`): Codex does not have an analogous self-nesting guard environment variable; no equivalent silent-exit happens when its env is inherited from a parent OpenClaw session. The Codex backend already has its own `clearEnv` list for auth/config keys (`auth-bridge.ts`, `config.ts`) and is not affected by this fix.
- OpenCode CLI: same as Codex — no documented nesting guard variable; no behavior change needed.
- Crabbox sandbox subprocess: runs in an isolated container and does not inherit the host `CLAUDECODE` env into a nested Claude run, so the silent-exit symptom does not surface there.
- Subagent spawn paths (`src/agents/cli-runner/execute.ts`): consume the backend `clearEnv` list, so they automatically pick up the new entries once `CLAUDE_CLI_CLEAR_ENV` is updated. No separate change required.

## Tests and validation

Which commands did you run?

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/anthropic/cli-shared.test.ts` (18 passed)
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/cli-backends.test.ts` (46 passed)
- `pnpm format:check` on touched files (0 errors)
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs` on touched files (0 errors)
- `pnpm dlx markdownlint-cli2 ... skills/coding-agent/SKILL.md` (0 errors)

What regression coverage was added or updated?

- `extensions/anthropic/cli-shared.test.ts`: new asserts for `CLAUDECODE`, `CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION`, `CLAUDE_CODE_ENTRYPOINT` in `CLAUDE_CLI_CLEAR_ENV` to lock in the nesting-guard strip.
- `src/agents/cli-backends.test.ts`: fixture `clearEnv` kept in parity with the production list so the simulated runtime backend entry reflects the same shape.

What failed before this fix, if known?

- No automated test failed. The visible failure was reporter's observation: 0/6 background dispatched Claude Code workers produced any output, because the inherited nesting guard caused Claude Code to exit silently.

If no test was added, why not?

- The skill-level recipe edit is documentation surface (markdown text in `skills/coding-agent/SKILL.md`); per AGENTS.md skill doc text changes do not require a new Vitest. The CLI backend change does have new test asserts; see above.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`) Yes — the coding-agent skill now ships a slightly different Claude Code launch command. Users dispatching workers via this skill on a fresh session will see `env -u ...` prefixed onto the bash command.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`) Yes (environment only) — three additional env vars (`CLAUDECODE`, `CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION`, `CLAUDE_CODE_ENTRYPOINT`) are now stripped from the managed Claude CLI child env. No config schema, defaults, or migrations changed.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`) No. The added strip targets are Claude Code's own nesting markers; they are not auth tokens, network endpoints, or tool-execution gates. No new permissions, no new network reachability.

What is the highest-risk area? Skill recipe wording — it is product-visible documentation copied into worker prompts. If the `env -u ...` syntax is mistyped users would hit a shell error instead of the silent exit they had before, which is observable and easy to diagnose.

How is that risk mitigated? Used the standard POSIX `env -u VAR` form (supported by macOS and Linux `env`); kept the rest of the command identical; added a hard-rule bullet that names the issue number so anyone editing the recipe later sees the rationale.

## Current review state

What is the next action? Maintainer review of the skill recipe wording and the `CLAUDE_CLI_CLEAR_ENV` additions.

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof? The end-to-end env-stripping mechanism is now proven with a real spawned child — see "Real behavior proof — child-env capture" below. The only thing still not exercised is an actual Claude Code *binary* started under a nested session (no Claude Code install here); the mechanism that fed the bug (inherited nesting vars in the child env) is now demonstrably neutralized.

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed? ClawSweeper's general "needs real behavior proof" ask is addressed by the child-env capture section below.

## Real behavior proof — child-env capture
ClawSweeper asked for real behavior proof. Below is a reproducible, executable proof that the env-stripping mechanism works end-to-end against the **real** production list and helpers — no Claude Code binary required, because the bug is purely that the nesting guard vars leak into the spawned child's environment. We prove they no longer leak.
**Script:** `qa/proofs/issue-57858-claude-nesting-env.mjs` (run with `npx tsx`).
**How real it is (production seams):**
- `CLAUDE_CLI_CLEAR_ENV` is imported verbatim from `extensions/anthropic/cli-shared.ts` — the exact list this PR edits. `extensions/anthropic/cli-backend.ts` wires it straight into the Claude backend as `clearEnv: [...CLAUDE_CLI_CLEAR_ENV]`.
- `sanitizeHostExecEnv` is imported verbatim from `src/infra/host-env-security.ts` — the real first step the production runner uses to derive the child env from `process.env` (`src/agents/cli-runner/execute.ts:395`). The proof shows it does **not** strip the three nesting vars on its own, so the `clearEnv` loop is provably what removes them.
- The `clearEnv` delete-loop at `src/agents/cli-runner/execute.ts:399-405` is an inline (non-exported) IIFE inside `executeCliRun()`, so it can't be imported; the proof mirrors **only** those few lines byte-for-byte, fed the real constant + real sanitized env, and asserts the applied list is identity-equal to the imported production constant.
**What it does and asserts:**
- **A** sets `CLAUDECODE=1`, `CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION=fake-session-abc123`, `CLAUDE_CODE_ENTRYPOINT=cli` in the parent (simulating OpenClaw running under Claude Code), plus a control var `OPENCLAW_PROOF_KEEPME=keep` that must survive.
- **B** builds the child env through the production path and prints the resolved list.
- **C** spawns a **real** child (`node -e ...` via `child_process.spawnSync`) with the production-built env and reads its env back: all three `CLAUDE_*` vars are `null`; `OPENCLAW_PROOF_KEEPME` is still `keep`.
- **D** negative control: spawns the same child with `env: process.env` (no strip) and shows `CLAUDECODE` IS `1` — proving the parent really carried it and the strip is what removes it (this is the bug reproduced).
- **E** asserts the `SKILL.md` `env -u CLAUDECODE -u CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION -u CLAUDE_CODE_ENTRYPOINT` recipe is present.

**Captured output** (`qa/proofs/issue-57858-claude-nesting-env.output.txt`, verbatim; user paths redacted):

```text
# Captured output of qa/proofs/issue-57858-claude-nesting-env.mjs
# Runner: npx tsx (node --experimental-strip-types cannot resolve the
#   openclaw/plugin-sdk/* tsconfig path alias that the real production module
#   transitively imports).
# Production seams imported verbatim: CLAUDE_CLI_CLEAR_ENV
#   (extensions/anthropic/cli-shared.ts) + sanitizeHostExecEnv
#   (src/infra/host-env-security.ts). Only the inline clearEnv delete-loop from
#   src/agents/cli-runner/execute.ts:399-405 is mirrored (it is a non-exported
#   IIFE), fed the real constant + real sanitized env; list identity is asserted.
# User-specific paths redacted (script prints only repo-relative content).

=== PART A: parent env (simulate OpenClaw running under Claude Code) ===
  parent CLAUDECODE              = 1
  parent CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION     = fake-session-abc123
  parent CLAUDE_CODE_ENTRYPOINT  = cli
  parent OPENCLAW_PROOF_KEEPME   = keep
  parent PATH present            = true

=== PART B: build child env through the PRODUCTION path ===
  imported CLAUDE_CLI_CLEAR_ENV (from extensions/anthropic/cli-shared.ts):
  ["ANTHROPIC_API_KEY","ANTHROPIC_API_KEY_OLD","ANTHROPIC_API_TOKEN","ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN","ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL","ANTHROPIC_CUSTOM_HEADERS","ANTHROPIC_OAUTH_TOKEN","ANTHROPIC_UNIX_SOCKET","CLAUDECODE","CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR","CLAUDE_CODE_API_KEY_FILE_DESCRIPTOR","CLAUDE_CODE_ENTRYPOINT","CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_REFRESH_TOKEN","CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_SCOPES","CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN","CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN_FILE_DESCRIPTOR","CLAUDE_CODE_PLUGIN_CACHE_DIR","CLAUDE_CODE_PLUGIN_SEED_DIR","CLAUDE_CODE_REMOTE","CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION","CLAUDE_CODE_USE_COWORK_PLUGINS","CLAUDE_CODE_USE_BEDROCK","CLAUDE_CODE_USE_FOUNDRY","CLAUDE_CODE_USE_VERTEX","OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT","OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_HEADERS","OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_LOGS_ENDPOINT","OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_LOGS_HEADERS","OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_LOGS_PROTOCOL","OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_METRICS_ENDPOINT","OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_METRICS_HEADERS","OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_METRICS_PROTOCOL","OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_PROTOCOL","OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_TRACES_ENDPOINT","OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_TRACES_HEADERS","OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_TRACES_PROTOCOL","OTEL_LOGS_EXPORTER","OTEL_METRICS_EXPORTER","OTEL_SDK_DISABLED","OTEL_TRACES_EXPORTER"]
  ok: CLAUDE_CLI_CLEAR_ENV contains CLAUDECODE (added by this PR)
  ok: CLAUDE_CLI_CLEAR_ENV contains CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION (added by this PR)
  ok: CLAUDE_CLI_CLEAR_ENV contains CLAUDE_CODE_ENTRYPOINT (pre-existing)
  ok: sanitizeHostExecEnv ALONE keeps CLAUDECODE -> clearEnv loop is the strip
  ok: applied clearEnv list is identity-equal to imported CLAUDE_CLI_CLEAR_ENV

=== PART C: spawn a REAL child with the production-built env ===
  child stdout (stripped env): {"CLAUDECODE":null,"CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION":null,"CLAUDE_CODE_ENTRYPOINT":null,"OPENCLAW_PROOF_KEEPME":"keep"}
  ok: child does NOT see CLAUDECODE
  ok: child does NOT see CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION
  ok: child does NOT see CLAUDE_CODE_ENTRYPOINT
  ok: child STILL sees unrelated OPENCLAW_PROOF_KEEPME=keep (strip is targeted)

=== PART D: negative control — spawn child WITHOUT stripping ===
  child stdout (raw process.env): {"CLAUDECODE":"1","CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION":"fake-session-abc123","CLAUDE_CODE_ENTRYPOINT":"cli","OPENCLAW_PROOF_KEEPME":"keep"}
  ok: without strip, child INHERITS CLAUDECODE=1 (proves parent carried it; reproduces the bug)
  ok: without strip, child INHERITS CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION (reproduces the bug)
  ok: without strip, child INHERITS CLAUDE_CODE_ENTRYPOINT (reproduces the bug)

=== PART E: assert the SKILL.md `env -u` recipe fix ===
  ok: SKILL.md contains an `env -u CLAUDECODE` line
  SKILL.md: - Claude Code: always prefix the launch with `env -u CLAUDECODE -u CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION -u CLAUDE_CODE_ENTRYPOINT` so a nested OpenClaw-inside-Claude-Code session does not inherit the parent's nesting guard and silently no-op the worker (issue #57858).
  SKILL.md: bash background:true workdir:/path/repo command:"env -u CLAUDECODE -u CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION -u CLAUDE_CODE_ENTRYPOINT claude --permission-mode bypassPermissions --print < \"$PROMPT\""
  ok: SKILL.md launch recipe prefixes the claude command with env -u
  ok: SKILL.md recipe strips all three nesting vars via env -u

ALL ASSERTIONS PASSED
exit=0
```

Narrative: the parent had all three nesting vars set; the production `CLAUDE_CLI_CLEAR_ENV` strip removed them from the spawned child while preserving unrelated vars (`OPENCLAW_PROOF_KEEPME`); the negative control confirms the parent actually carried `CLAUDECODE=1` (so absence in the stripped child is caused by the strip, not by the var never being set); and the `SKILL.md` `env -u` recipe is asserted present. `ALL ASSERTIONS PASSED`, exit 0.
